author: plattnum
description: Visual time tracking with infinite-scroll timeline, hierarchical reports, and one-click invoicing. Data stays in your vault as markdown. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.description: Visual time tracking with infinite-scroll timeline, hierarchical reports, and one-click invoicing. Data stays in your vault as markdown.
manifest.name: Where Did The Time Go

name: Where Did The Time Go

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new

- **Day total in the timeline header** — when a single day fills the viewport, the header now shows that day's logged total inline next to the date. It updates live as you add, edit, or delete entries. Multi-day views omit it.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Removed `?` from the plugin display name to satisfy Obsidian's community directory automated name validator. No functional changes from 1.0.6.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new in 1.0.6

### 🧹 Code health

- **All `!important` removed from styles.css.** Replaced with proper selector-specificity wins everywhere. The `.is-hidden` utility now uses a doubled class (`.is-hidden.is-hidden`) to win against component-level rules without raising weight. Zero visual change for users.
- Removed a dead duplicate `.time-input-error` rule block left over from an older refactor — the modal-scoped rule has always been the active one. Zero visual change.

---

**Full diff:** [1.0.5...1.0.6](https://github.com/plattnum/where-did-the-time-go/compare/1.0.5...1.0.6)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new in 1.0.5

### ✨ Improved

- **Overlap warning banner now adapts to your theme.** Previously the "this entry overlaps with another" banner was a fixed Tailwind-red regardless of theme. It now uses Obsidian's `--text-error` color, so it stays legible in light, dark, and custom themes.

### 🧹 Code health

- Removed an orphan CSS block for the older `.client-setting` settings UI (replaced by `.client-card` expandable cards in a previous refactor). No visual change — purely dead-code cleanup.
- Merged duplicate `.time-tracker-timeline` and `.time-tracker-reports` selectors (variables and layout were declared in separate adjacent rules — now consolidated). No visual change.
- Cleared four duplicate-selector warnings from the `plugin-review` scanner for `.overlap-warning-banner/icon/messages/message`.

### 🔐 Provenance

- Release assets continue to carry GitHub artifact attestations (added in 1.0.4). Verify a download came from this repo:
  ```bash
  gh attestation verify main.js --repo plattnum/where-did-the-time-go
  ```

---

**Full diff:** [1.0.4...1.0.5](https://github.com/plattnum/where-did-the-time-go/compare/1.0.4...1.0.5)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new in 1.0.4

### 🐛 Fixed

- **Drag-to-create reliability.** Short drags (the most common case: dragging across two 15-minute grid lines to make a 15-min entry) silently aborted ~50% of the time. The threshold check now snaps to the grid *before* deciding whether the drag was big enough — so dragging between any two grid lines reliably opens the create modal.

### ✨ Improved

- **Popped-out windows now work properly.** If you drag an existing entry to move or resize it in a popped-out timeline window, it now updates correctly instead of going nowhere. CSV/JSON export in a popped-out Reports view also works as expected.

### ⬆️ Compatibility

- **`minAppVersion` is now 1.7.2** (was 0.15.0). The plugin uses several Obsidian APIs newer than the old minimum — most notably `Workspace.revealLeaf` (1.7.2), `Vault.createFolder` (1.4.0), and `ButtonComponent.setTooltip` (1.1.0). If you're on Obsidian 1.7.2 or newer (released late 2024), you're fine.

### 🔐 Infrastructure

- Release assets (`main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css`) now carry **GitHub artifact attestations** signed via sigstore. Verify a download came from this repo:
  ```bash
  gh attestation verify main.js --repo plattnum/where-did-the-time-go
  ```

### 🧹 Code health

- Cleared all `plugin-review` scanner warnings: `createSpan` in the invoice modal, `createEl('a')` and `activeDocument` in the Reports and Timeline views, and a stray unused parameter in settings. No user-visible effect — just hygiene.

---

**Full diff:** [1.0.3...1.0.4](https://github.com/plattnum/where-did-the-time-go/compare/1.0.3...1.0.4)

What's New

- Daily time totals in timeline - Each day header now shows the total logged hours for that day as a badge, so you can see at a glance how much time you've billed without navigating to Reports.
